# numhelpr

`numhelpr` is an R package that provides simple helper functions for
common number formatting and math tasks that can be annoying to do in
base R.

## Installation

You can install the development version of `numhelpr` from GitHub with:

``` r

remotes::install_github("ADC-405-S26/numhelpr")
```

## Functions

### `percent()`

Converts a decimal value into a formatted percentage string.

``` r

library(numhelpr)
percent(0.753)
percent(c(0.1, 0.25, 0.873))
```

### `percent_change()`

Calculates the percentage change between two values.

``` r

percent_change(10, 12.5)
percent_change(c(10, 25, 5), c(12.5, 22, 5.75))
```

### `clamp()`

Restricts numeric values to a specified range.

``` r

clamp(c(1, 5, 10, 15, 20), lower = 5, upper = 15)
```

## Data

The package includes a built-in dataset `sales_data` that can be used to
practice the functions.

``` r

sales_data
```

# Package index

## All functions

- [`clamp()`](https://adc-405-s26.github.io/numhelpr/reference/clamp.md)
  : Clamp Numeric Values Between a Minimum and Maximum
- [`percent()`](https://adc-405-s26.github.io/numhelpr/reference/percent.md)
  : Format a Decimal as a Percentage
- [`percent_change()`](https://adc-405-s26.github.io/numhelpr/reference/percent_change.md)
  : Calculate Percent Change Between Two Values
- [`sales_data`](https://adc-405-s26.github.io/numhelpr/reference/sales_data.md)
  : Sales Data for numhelpr Examples
